message:
fix: witness startup fails with exit 127 due to env/exec ordering

The TOML start_command "exec claude ..." was being prepended with
"env -u CLAUDECODE NODE_OPTIONS='' ", producing:
  env -u CLAUDECODE NODE_OPTIONS='' exec claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

Since "exec" is a shell builtin (not a binary), env(1) tries to run
"exec" as a program and fails with status 127. The fix reorders to:
  exec env -u CLAUDECODE NODE_OPTIONS='' claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

This matches the identical fix already in daemon/lifecycle.go:478-483.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -41,3 +41,7 @@ external_projects:
 
 # Custom issue types for Gas Town (fallback when database is unavailable)
 types.custom: "agent,role,rig,convoy,slot,queue,event,message,molecule,gate,merge-request"
+
+prefix: 
+issue-prefix: 
+dolt.idle-timeout: "0"
